items.subjective_preview: Patient presents for GERD follow-up. Reports persistent heartburn despite taking omeprazole 20mg daily for the past 8 weeks, taken 30 minutes before b...
items.objective_preview: Vitals: BP 120/76 mmHg, HR 74 bpm, Temp 98.4F, Wt 155 lbs. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No organomegaly. Bowel sounds normal. No epigastr...
items.assessment: Gastroesophageal reflux disease - refractory to standard-dose PPI monotherapy
items.plan:
1. Increase omeprazole to 40mg BID (before breakfast and dinner)
2. Reinforce dietary modifications - avoid citrus, tomatoes, coffee, chocolate, alcohol
3. Continue elevating head of bed
4. If no improvement in 4 weeks, refer to GI for upper endoscopy consideration
5. Referral to Dr. O'Brien (GI) submitted for endoscopy evaluation
6. Follow-up in 4-6 weeks
